message.content: Add rate limiting to the public API endpoints.

content.text: Starting with a token-bucket implemented in application middleware.

input.content:
// sketch: in-process token bucket per client id
export function tokenBucket() { /* ... */ }

content.text: Actually, scrap that. In-process token buckets won't coordinate across our autoscaled fleet — each pod would have its own bucket and a client could get N*limit effective throughput. Deleting the sketch and switching to an edge-layer approach.

Going with Cloudflare's rate-limiting rules at the edge instead. It enforces limits before the request ever reaches our pods, and the count is globally consistent because it runs on a single logical layer in front of the fleet. Configuring 100 req/min per IP on /api/public/*.
